summary_one_line: A context ID byte must NOT equal the mesh-local-prefix constant and must instead match a context ID embedded in a parsed PrefixTlv/ContextTlv.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input must supply an aContextId byte distinct from Mle::kMeshLocalPrefixContextId (so the line-177 equality is false) while carrying a well-formed PrefixTlv->ContextTlv whose embedded context ID equals that same byte, letting the loop at line 183/187 match and resolve the false-branch path.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds carry concentrated, structured TLV-shaped runs (e.g. the fd-de-ad-00-be-ef mesh-local-prefix-like bytes at 0x19-0x1e present in every W seed) and resolve the false branch in 10/10 trials, driving the line-183 TlvIterate loop (W T=0 F=45 at line 183) and the IsOnMesh path (45 W hits) rather than GetContextForMeshLocalPrefix.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds take the true branch (aContextId == magic), invoking GetContextForMeshLocalPrefix (47 L hits, 0 W hits) and never entering the TLV-iteration loop; naive blocks the false side in 8/10 trials because random havoc rarely lands the byte that makes the equality false yet still matches an embedded ContextTlv id.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver entry from the line-177/line-187 integer compares and substitutes the recorded other-operand (the context ID seen in the parsed TLV) into the aContextId offset, solving the relational equality that naive's havoc/token stack cannot target directly.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is an equality test of aContextId against the mesh-local-prefix context constant, and the globally blocked side is false.
The false-branch path leads into the PrefixTlv iteration loop that matches an embedded context ID against aContextId.
Winner cmplog seeds resolve the false branch in all 10 trials while loser naive blocks it in 8/10.
Winner seeds carry a concentrated mesh-local-prefix-like byte run absent in losers, evidencing structured TLV content.
The winner drives the TLV-iteration loop while the loser takes the mesh-local-prefix path, shown by inverted line-177 branch counts.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: T=0 F=45  T=31 F=0  if (aContextId == Mle::kMeshLocalPrefixContextId)

evidence_trail.claim: GetContextForMeshLocalPrefix is invoked only by the loser, confirming the true/false role split.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a recorded CMP operand into matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S capability, only havoc and token mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 13390 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 09d1aa462b9734c9` and observed 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 9 of the 10-level ancestor chain; tool printed 'I2S-floor signal: 1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 9.'

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where aContextId is compared to a runtime-derived context ID under cmplog still shows no cmplog>naive advantage on the false branch, or naive resolves the false branch as fast as cmplog at equal budget.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact dataflow link from the concentrated 0x19-0x1e byte run to the aContextId compare operand is inferred from source structure rather than directly traced through the parser.
